name: BPC-157
overview: Body Protection Compound 157 is a stable gastric pentadecapeptide known for its ability to accelerate healing of tendons, ligaments, muscle tissue, and gut. It is particularly popular among athletes and individuals recovering from injuries seeking to optimize tissue repair and reduce inflammation.
mechanism: BPC-157 functions by upregulating growth hormone receptor expression, promoting angiogenesis through new blood vessel formation, and modulating nitric oxide production. These mechanisms work synergistically to enhance protein synthesis, reduce inflammatory cytokines, and improve blood flow to damaged tissues, facilitating rapid repair processes.
effects: Reduced inflammation at injury sites, accelerated tissue repair and regeneration, improved joint comfort and mobility, and potential gut healing benefits including improved digestion and intestinal permeability. Systemic effects extend beyond the injection site.
results_timeline: Joint and tendon improvements are often noticed within 2–4 weeks of consistent use. Gut health effects can appear more rapidly, sometimes within days to one week of starting.
timing: Typically administered in 2 daily injections for consistent tissue exposure. Timing relative to meals is not critical. Can be injected near the site of injury for localized benefit, or systemically via abdominal SubQ.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: 250–500 mcg (5–10 units) per injection, 1–2 times daily. A 4–12 week cycle is typical. Many users split the daily dose into two injections (morning and evening) for consistent tissue exposure. At 5 mg/mL: 0.25 mg = 5 units, 0.5 mg = 10 units.

name: TB-500
overview: Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500) is a synthetic version of a naturally occurring protein present in human cells. It works systemically throughout the body and is frequently stacked with BPC-157 for comprehensive recovery support, offering broader whole-body tissue repair benefits.
mechanism: TB-500 promotes cell migration and angiogenesis through actin polymerization, facilitating improved blood flow and tissue repair across multiple systems. Unlike localized peptides, it distributes systemically — making it effective for whole-body recovery, flexibility improvements, and addressing inflammation in multiple locations simultaneously.
effects: Significant reduction in inflammation and soreness, improved flexibility and range of motion, enhanced systemic tissue repair and recovery, and anecdotally reported improvements in hair growth and skin quality. Effects tend to be whole-body rather than localized.
results_timeline: Noticeable improvements in flexibility and reduced post-workout soreness are typically observed within 2–3 weeks of consistent use. Full systemic benefits develop gradually over 4–6 weeks.
timing: Injected twice weekly during the loading phase (4–6 weeks), then once weekly for maintenance. SubQ injection anywhere on the body — site-specific injection is not necessary as TB-500 distributes systemically.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Loading phase: 2–2.5 mg (40–50 units) twice per week for 4–6 weeks. Maintenance phase: 2–2.5 mg (40–50 units) once per week thereafter. This strategy builds therapeutic levels quickly while sustaining benefits with reduced injection frequency. At 5 mg/mL: 2 mg = 40 units, 2.5 mg = 50 units.

name: Glow Blend
overview: Glow is a pre-formulated blend of GHK-Cu, BPC-157, and TB-500 — three peptides with complementary mechanisms targeting skin rejuvenation, collagen synthesis, tissue repair, and systemic anti-inflammatory support. It is designed for users seeking comprehensive aesthetic and recovery benefits from a single preparation.
mechanism: GHK-Cu drives collagen and elastin production and supports microvascular repair for skin and connective tissue. BPC-157 modulates nitric oxide signaling and growth factor expression for localized and systemic tissue repair. TB-500 promotes cell migration and actin polymerization for systemic healing and flexibility improvements. Together they address skin quality, joint health, and inflammation through three complementary pathways.
effects: Improved skin firmness, texture, and tone; reduced appearance of fine lines; improved hair follicle environment; enhanced tendon and joint recovery; systemic anti-inflammatory support; improved microcirculation delivering better nutrient flow to skin and connective tissue.
results_timeline: Week 1–2: improved skin hydration and reduced irritation. Weeks 2–4: joint comfort improving. Weeks 3–6: skin firmness and tone improve. Weeks 4–8: collagen-related changes more visible.
timing: Skin and anti-aging focus: 1–2 mg daily or 5× per week. Recovery focus: 2–4 mg daily for 3–4 weeks, then drop to 1–2 mg 2–3× weekly for maintenance.

doses.label: 70mg
doses.dosage_text: Skin / anti-aging focus: 1–2 mg (5–10 units) daily or 5× per week. Recovery / injury focus: 2–4 mg (10–17 units) daily for 3–4 weeks. Maintenance: 1–2 mg (5–10 units) 2–3× per week. At 23.3 mg/mL: 1 mg ≈ 5 units, 2 mg ≈ 10 units.

name: KLOW Blend
overview: KLOW is a pre-formulated blend of GHK-Cu, KPV, and BPC-157 that targets the gut-skin-recovery triad. The inclusion of KPV — a tripeptide derived from alpha-MSH with potent gut-protective and anti-inflammatory effects — distinguishes it from other blends by adding a gut health dimension alongside skin and tissue repair.
mechanism: GHK-Cu stimulates collagen synthesis and microvascular repair for skin and tissue health. KPV (Lys-Pro-Val) modulates inflammatory cytokine signaling in gut epithelial cells, supports gut barrier function, and reduces systemic inflammatory load through alpha-MSH derived pathways. BPC-157 promotes tissue repair via growth factor modulation and angiogenesis. Together they address the interconnected gut-skin-recovery axis.
effects: Improved skin texture and appearance, support for gut barrier function and digestive comfort, reduced systemic inflammatory response, enhanced recovery from training or illness, and overall wellness through the gut-skin connection.
results_timeline: Week 1–2: improved hydration and recovery feel. Weeks 2–4: better digestion and reduced gut irritation. Weeks 3–6: improvements in skin appearance and texture. Weeks 4–8: more noticeable collagen and recovery-related benefits.
timing: General wellness: 1–2 mg daily or 5× per week. Recovery / inflammation: 2–4 mg daily for 3–6 weeks, followed by 1–2 mg maintenance 2–3× weekly.

doses.label: 80mg
doses.dosage_text: Skin / gut / wellness focus: 1–2 mg (4–8 units) daily or 5× per week. Recovery / inflammation focus: 2–4 mg (8–15 units) daily for 3–6 weeks. Maintenance: 1–2 mg (4–8 units) 2–3× per week. At 26.7 mg/mL: 1 mg ≈ 4 units, 2 mg ≈ 8 units.

name: GHK-Cu
overview: GHK-Cu (Copper Peptide GHK) is a naturally occurring copper-binding tripeptide found throughout the body, essential for collagen synthesis, wound healing, and tissue remodeling. It is widely used for skin rejuvenation, hair follicle support, and connective tissue repair, making it popular in aesthetic and recovery applications.
mechanism: GHK-Cu activates collagen and elastin synthesis in fibroblasts, promotes angiogenesis for improved microvascular repair, and regulates matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) involved in tissue remodeling. Its copper-binding function supports enzymatic activity in collagen cross-linking and antioxidant pathways, while anti-inflammatory signaling reduces tissue degradation and promotes healing.
effects: Improved skin firmness, elasticity, and texture; reduced appearance of fine lines over time; improved hair follicle environment and potential scalp circulation support; accelerated wound healing and scar remodeling; systemic anti-inflammatory effects with improved nutrient delivery to skin and connective tissue.
results_timeline: Week 1–2: improved skin hydration and reduced irritation. Weeks 2–4: tone and texture begin noticeably improving. Weeks 4–8: collagen-related firmness and more visible structural skin changes.
timing: Can be used any time of day. Daily or 5× per week is standard. Start low for the first week to gauge tolerance — GHK-Cu can cause mild stinging at the injection site.

doses.label: 100mg
doses.dosage_text: Skin / cosmetic: 0.5–2 mg (1.5–6 units) daily or 5× per week. Tissue repair cycles: 2–5 mg (6–15 units) daily for 2–4 weeks, then drop to 1–2 mg 2–3× weekly. Note: small unit changes equal significant dose jumps at this concentration — dose carefully. At 33.3 mg/mL: 1 mg = 3 units, 2 mg = 6 units, 5 mg = 15 units.

name: Ipamorelin
overview: Ipamorelin is a selective growth hormone secretagogue peptide that stimulates pulsatile GH release from the pituitary without significantly affecting cortisol, prolactin, or appetite. Its high selectivity makes it a preferred choice for recovery support, sleep quality improvement, and body composition research.
mechanism: Ipamorelin acts as a ghrelin receptor agonist and GHRP (growth hormone releasing peptide), binding to specific receptors in the pituitary and hypothalamus to trigger GH secretion. Unlike other GHRPs, it shows high receptor selectivity, producing GH pulses without the cortisol or appetite-stimulating side effects associated with less selective secretagogues.
effects: Improved sleep quality and depth, enhanced recovery between training sessions, support for joint and connective tissue health, potential improvements in body composition with consistent use, and mild anti-aging effects associated with optimized GH levels.
results_timeline: Sleep improvements are often noticed within 1–2 weeks. Recovery benefits develop over 3–6 weeks. Body composition changes require 8–12 weeks of consistent use.
timing: Best injected before bed or in a fasted state (morning or between meals). Avoid injection within 1 hour of a high-carbohydrate meal — insulin can blunt GH response.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Typical research range: 0.1–0.3 mg per administration (2–6 units). Recovery / repair: 0.1–0.2 mg (2–4 units). Sleep / GH support: 0.1–0.3 mg (2–6 units) before bed. Some research setups explore multiple smaller doses (e.g. 0.1 mg AM fasted + 0.1 mg before bed). At 5 mg/mL: 0.1 mg = 2 units, 0.2 mg = 4 units, 0.3 mg = 6 units.

name: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin
overview: A pre-blended combination of CJC-1295 (no DAC) and Ipamorelin at equal ratio, designed to amplify natural GH pulses through dual-pathway stimulation. CJC-1295 extends the duration of GH release while Ipamorelin triggers the pulse, creating a synergistic effect stronger than either peptide alone.
mechanism: CJC-1295 (no DAC) acts as a GHRH analogue, signaling the pituitary to prepare and release GH. Ipamorelin acts simultaneously as a GHRP, mimicking ghrelin to trigger GH secretion through a complementary receptor pathway. Their dual-mechanism synergy produces stronger, more sustained GH pulses than either compound alone, while maintaining selectivity and avoiding cortisol elevation.
effects: Boosted natural GH pulses, improved sleep quality and recovery, enhanced fat metabolism over time, faster tissue repair, joint and connective tissue support, and general anti-aging effects on skin and body composition.
results_timeline: Week 1–2: improved sleep. Weeks 2–4: fat loss begins and energy improves. Weeks 3–6: recovery enhancement. Weeks 6–10: visible physique and body composition changes.
timing: Best before bed or in the morning fasted. Avoid carbohydrates and fats for 60–90 minutes after injection for optimal GH response. 1:1 blend means 1 mg total = 0.5 mg CJC + 0.5 mg Ipamorelin.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Anti-aging / recovery / sleep: 0.25–0.5 mg (5–10 units) before bed. Fat loss / performance: 1 mg (20 units) once or twice daily (AM fasted + bedtime). Remember: 1 mg total = 0.5 mg CJC-1295 + 0.5 mg Ipamorelin. At 5 mg/mL: 0.25 mg = 5 units, 0.5 mg = 10 units, 1 mg = 20 units.

name: Sermorelin
overview: Sermorelin is a synthetic analogue of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) that stimulates the pituitary gland to produce and secrete natural GH. It is commonly studied for anti-aging support, fat loss, sleep quality, and recovery — preserving the body's natural GH feedback regulation rather than replacing it.
mechanism: Sermorelin binds to GHRH receptors in the anterior pituitary, triggering pulsatile GH secretion in a physiologically regulated manner. Unlike exogenous GH administration, sermorelin preserves the body's natural feedback loop — GH release remains self-limiting and pulsatile, which is considered more physiologic and avoids the desensitization seen with constant GH exposure.
effects: Improved sleep quality and depth, enhanced body composition over time, increased energy and vitality, improved skin quality, enhanced recovery from training, and potential reduction in visceral fat with extended use.
results_timeline: Sleep improvements often noticed within 1–2 weeks. Meaningful fat loss and body composition changes develop over 6–12 weeks of consistent use.
timing: Best injected at night before bed or in the morning fasted. Avoid after high-carbohydrate meals as elevated insulin can blunt GH release.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Typical range: 0.2–0.5 mg daily (4–10 units). Fat loss / recovery: 0.2–0.5 mg daily. Anti-aging / sleep: 0.2–0.3 mg (4–6 units) before bed. Higher experimental dosing: 0.5–1 mg daily. At 5 mg/mL: 0.2 mg = 4 units, 0.3 mg = 6 units, 0.5 mg = 10 units.

name: Tesamorelin
overview: Tesamorelin is a stabilized synthetic analogue of GHRH developed specifically for visceral fat reduction. It is FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy and is studied more broadly for abdominal fat loss and metabolic support, driving results through the GH/IGF-1 axis rather than appetite suppression.
mechanism: Tesamorelin binds to GHRH receptors in the pituitary, stimulating GH secretion which in turn elevates IGF-1 levels. The resulting GH/IGF-1 signal drives lipolysis, with particularly pronounced effects on visceral adipose tissue in the abdominal region. Unlike raw GH administration, it maintains natural pulsatility and preserves feedback regulation.
effects: Reduction in visceral and abdominal fat, improvements in body composition, potential improvements in lipid profiles, and general effects associated with optimized GH levels including enhanced recovery and improved skin quality.
results_timeline: Visible reduction in abdominal fat is typically noted within 4–8 weeks of consistent use. Full body composition changes develop over 3–6 months.
timing: Best injected before bed or in the morning fasted. Avoid after high-carbohydrate meals to maximize GH response. Consistent daily dosing is recommended for optimal results.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Fat loss / visceral fat: 0.5–2 mg daily (10–40 units). Most research uses 1–2 mg per day. Best results seen with consistent daily dosing over 3–6 months. At 5 mg/mL: 0.5 mg = 10 units, 1 mg = 20 units, 2 mg = 40 units.

name: Semaglutide
overview: Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist originally developed for type 2 diabetes management that has become one of the most effective researched agents for sustained fat loss. It works by powerfully suppressing appetite and slowing gastric emptying, enabling consistent caloric reduction with minimal conscious effort.
mechanism: Semaglutide mimics the glucagon-like peptide-1 hormone by binding to GLP-1 receptors throughout the body. It increases glucose-dependent insulin secretion, decreases glucagon release, slows gastric emptying to extend satiety, and activates central nervous system pathways in the hypothalamus that reduce appetite and food cravings. Its long half-life allows once-weekly dosing.
effects: Profound appetite suppression, dramatically reduced food cravings, significant reduction in caloric intake with minimal conscious effort, accelerated fat loss (10–20% body weight in clinical trials), improved blood sugar control, and increased metabolic efficiency.
results_timeline: Appetite suppression is felt within the first week. Meaningful and visible fat loss typically becomes apparent by weeks 4–8 of consistent use. Full metabolic effects and maximum fat loss develop over 3–6 months.
timing: Once weekly, same day each week. Subcutaneous injection in the abdomen, thigh, or love handles. Food timing does not matter. Slow titration is critical to minimize nausea — do not rush the ramp.

doses.label: 20mg
doses.dosage_text: Start at 0.25 mg (~2.5 units) weekly and titrate slowly to minimize nausea. Most fat-loss benefit is achieved between 1–2.4 mg weekly. At 10 mg/mL: 0.25 mg = 2.5 units, 0.5 mg = 5 units, 1 mg = 10 units, 1.7 mg = 17 units, 2.4 mg = 24 units.

name: Tirzepatide
overview: Tirzepatide is a dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist that produces significant fat loss and metabolic improvements. By simultaneously activating both receptor systems, it achieves stronger appetite suppression and metabolic effects than GLP-1-only peptides, with clinical data showing 15–20% body weight reduction.
mechanism: GLP-1 agonism activates satiety centers in the hypothalamus, slows gastric emptying to extend fullness, and increases glucose-dependent insulin secretion. GIP agonism complements this by improving insulin sensitivity, reducing glucagon levels, and enhancing fat tissue energy metabolism. The combined dual-receptor effect produces potent, sustained appetite control with favorable metabolic outcomes.
effects: Significant appetite suppression and reduced food cravings, consistent fat loss with proper titration, improved blood sugar regulation, enhanced metabolic efficiency. Side effects (primarily nausea) are greatly minimized with slow dose titration.
results_timeline: Appetite suppression noticeable in weeks 1–4. Meaningful fat loss visible by weeks 4–8. Maximum fat loss and metabolic effects develop over 3–6 months at maintenance dose.
timing: Once weekly, same day each week. Subcutaneous injection in the abdomen, thigh, or love handles. Food timing does not affect efficacy. Titrate slowly — never skip titration steps.

doses.label: 20mg
doses.dosage_text: Begin at 2.5 mg (25 units) weekly for weeks 1–4 and titrate upward. Most fat-loss benefit at 5–12.5 mg weekly. At 10 mg/mL: 2.5 mg = 25 units, 5 mg = 50 units, 7.5 mg = 75 units, 10 mg = 100 units.

doses.label: 30mg
doses.dosage_text: Begin at 2.5 mg (~17 units) weekly for weeks 1–4 and titrate upward. Most fat-loss benefit at 5–12.5 mg weekly. At 15 mg/mL: 2.5 mg ≈ 17 units, 5 mg ≈ 33 units, 7.5 mg ≈ 50 units, 10 mg ≈ 67 units.

name: Retatrutide
overview: Retatrutide is a next-generation triple receptor agonist targeting GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously. In clinical trials it has demonstrated fat loss outcomes exceeding other peptides in its class, with some participants achieving 20%+ body weight reduction — driven in part by its unique thermogenic glucagon component.
mechanism: GLP-1 agonism reduces appetite and slows gastric emptying. GIP agonism improves insulin sensitivity and fat metabolism. Glucagon receptor activation increases basal energy expenditure and promotes fat oxidation — adding a thermogenic dimension absent in dual-receptor agonists. The triple-receptor effect amplifies fat loss through both appetite reduction and increased energy expenditure simultaneously.
effects: Profound appetite suppression, accelerated fat loss exceeding other GLP-1-class peptides, improved blood sugar regulation, potential increases in basal energy expenditure via glucagon activation, and improvements in metabolic health markers.
results_timeline: Week 1–2: reduced appetite and fewer cravings. Weeks 2–4: improved portion control and satiety. Weeks 4–8: consistent weight loss and improved energy. Weeks 8–12+: more noticeable body composition changes and continued appetite control.
timing: Once per week, same day each week. Subcutaneous injection in the abdomen, thigh, or love handles. Timing relative to meals is not critical. Start conservatively and increase only after tolerating lower doses.

doses.label: 15mg
doses.dosage_text: Conservative start: 0.5–1 mg (5–10 units) once weekly. Fat loss / appetite support: 1–3 mg (10–30 units) once weekly. Advanced: 3–5 mg (30–50 units) once weekly (for those who have tolerated lower doses). At 10 mg/mL: 0.5 mg = 5 units, 1 mg = 10 units, 2 mg = 20 units, 3 mg = 30 units.

doses.label: 30mg
doses.dosage_text: Conservative start: 0.5–1 mg (5–10 units) once weekly. Fat loss / appetite support: 1–3 mg (10–30 units) once weekly. Advanced: 3–5 mg (30–50 units) once weekly. Most fat-loss benefit at 2–4 mg weekly — higher doses increase nausea risk without proportional benefit. At 10 mg/mL: 1 mg = 10 units, 2 mg = 20 units, 4 mg = 40 units.

name: MOTS-c
overview: MOTS-c is a mitochondria-derived peptide (MDP) encoded within mitochondrial DNA that acts as a metabolic regulator. It improves cellular energy efficiency, insulin sensitivity, and exercise performance at the mitochondrial level — effectively mimicking many of the metabolic benefits of exercise at the cellular level.
mechanism: MOTS-c activates AMPK (AMP-activated protein kinase), the master cellular energy sensor, and modulates metabolic pathways including glucose uptake, fatty acid oxidation, and mitochondrial stress response. It improves metabolic flexibility — the body's ability to switch between fuel sources — and activates stress resistance pathways linked to healthy aging and longevity biology.
effects: Improved energy levels and sustained endurance, enhanced metabolic flexibility and insulin sensitivity, better glucose utilization, improved body composition during exercise protocols, cleaner energy without stimulant effects, and potential support for longevity-related pathways via AMPK activation.
results_timeline: Week 1: noticeable energy shift. Weeks 2–3: improved workout stamina. Weeks 3–5: body composition changes begin. Weeks 4–6: metabolic rhythm stabilizes.
timing: Often taken earlier in the day as it can feel energizing. Some research users time it with training days for enhanced exercise effect. Many protocols run 4–6 weeks on, then take a break.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Performance / endurance: 2.5–5 mg (50–100 units), 2× per week. Conservative start: 2.5 mg (50 units) 2× per week. Most protocols run 4–6 weeks on, then take a break. At 5 mg/mL: 0.5 mg = 10 units, 1 mg = 20 units, 2.5 mg = 50 units.

name: NAD+
overview: NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide) is a critical coenzyme present in every cell, essential for energy metabolism, DNA repair, and cellular signaling. Injectable NAD+ allows direct systemic delivery, bypassing the digestive conversion limitations of oral supplementation for higher bioavailability.
mechanism: NAD+ serves as a substrate for sirtuins (SIRT1–7), which regulate metabolism, inflammation, and longevity pathways. It is essential for the citric acid cycle and oxidative phosphorylation (ATP production), and supports PARP enzymes involved in DNA repair. Systemic NAD+ levels naturally decline with age, and supplementation aims to restore cellular energy and repair capacity.
effects: Enhanced cellular energy production, improved cognitive function and mental clarity, support for DNA repair processes, improved metabolism, potential neuroprotective effects, improved recovery, and a general increase in vitality. Higher doses are more stimulating and are reserved for performance and cognitive applications.
results_timeline: Energy and cognitive effects can often be felt within the first injection session. Cumulative metabolic and repair benefits develop over several weeks of consistent use.
timing: Best earlier in the day as it can be stimulating. 3–5× per week is a standard research protocol. Start at low doses (10–20 mg) and assess tolerance before increasing — NAD+ can feel intense at higher doses.

doses.label: 1000mg
doses.dosage_text: Low / daily support: 10–20 mg (5–10 units), 3–5× per week. Energy / cognitive push: 20–50 mg (10–25 units). Go slow — NAD+ can feel intense. At 200 mg/mL: 10 mg = 5 units, 20 mg = 10 units, 50 mg = 25 units.

name: Glutathione
overview: Glutathione is the body's master antioxidant — a tripeptide synthesized from glutamate, cysteine, and glycine present in every cell. Injectable glutathione bypasses digestive breakdown, delivering high concentrations systemically for antioxidant support, detoxification, immune function, and skin brightening.
mechanism: Glutathione neutralizes reactive oxygen species and free radicals, regenerates vitamins C and E, and supports Phase II liver detoxification. It is central to immune cell function and anti-inflammatory regulation. At higher doses, it modulates melanin synthesis by inhibiting tyrosinase activity, contributing to its well-documented skin-brightening effect.
effects: Powerful antioxidant and detox support, reduced oxidative stress and cellular damage, enhanced immune function, improved recovery from training and illness, and skin brightening with more even tone at higher doses over extended use.
results_timeline: Antioxidant effects are immediate at the cellular level. Visible skin brightening typically requires several weeks of consistent higher-dose use. Recovery and immune benefits accumulate over weeks of regular use.
timing: Best post-workout or in the morning. Pairs well with NAD+ for a combined energy and recovery protocol. 2–4× per week is typical.

doses.label: 1500mg
doses.dosage_text: General wellness / antioxidant: 50–100 mg (15–30 units) 2–4× per week. Skin brightening / higher antioxidant push: 100–200 mg (30–70 units). Pairs well with 10–25 units of NAD+. At 300 mg/mL: 50 mg = 17 units, 100 mg = 33 units, 150 mg = 50 units.

name: Semax
overview: Semax is a synthetic analogue of ACTH (adrenocorticotropic hormone) developed in Russia and studied extensively for cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and stress resilience. It is one of the most well-researched nootropic peptides, with clinical applications in stroke recovery and cognitive impairment research.
mechanism: Semax enhances BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor) and NGF (nerve growth factor) levels in the brain, modulates dopamine and serotonin neurotransmitter systems, and supports neuroplasticity. It also improves cerebral blood circulation and has neuroprotective effects, supporting both acute cognitive performance and long-term brain health through multiple pathways.
effects: Enhanced focus and mental clarity, improved memory encoding and recall, increased mental energy and motivation without stimulant crash, improved stress resilience, and neuroprotective support for brain circulation and cognitive recovery. Effects are often described as 'clear-headed drive' rather than stimulation.
results_timeline: First dose: mental clarity often noticed within hours. Week 1: consistent focus improvement. Weeks 2–3: memory and learning feel easier and more efficient. Weeks 3–5: stable cognitive performance baseline established.
timing: Best in the morning or before mentally demanding work. Can be taken before training for a focus enhancement effect. Avoid late evening as it can be stimulating. Use 1–2× daily.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Focus / productivity / cognitive boost: 0.3–0.6 mg (10–20 units) 1–2× daily. Memory / learning / neuro-support: 0.6–1 mg (20–30 units) morning or pre-task. Conservative start: 0.17 mg (5 units) once daily for the first few days. At 3.33 mg/mL: 0.17 mg = 5 units, 0.33 mg = 10 units, 0.5 mg = 15 units, 1 mg = 30 units.

name: Selank
overview: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide analogue of the endogenous immunomodulatory peptide Tuftsin, developed in Russia. It is studied for anxiolytic, nootropic, and mood-stabilizing effects without sedation, dependence potential, or cognitive impairment — making it distinct from pharmaceutical anxiolytics.
mechanism: Selank modulates GABA receptor expression, serotonin transport activity, and BDNF levels in the hippocampus. It also exerts immunomodulatory effects by influencing cytokine expression. Unlike benzodiazepines, Selank does not directly bind GABA-A receptors, which explains its anxiolytic effect without sedation, tolerance, or withdrawal.
effects: Reduced anxiety and acute stress response, improved mood stability and emotional resilience, enhanced cognitive function including memory and focus, calm mental clarity without drowsiness or cognitive impairment. Effects are calming rather than sedating.
results_timeline: First dose: anxiety reduction may be felt within hours. Week 1: improved stress tolerance and mood baseline. Weeks 2–3: consistent cognitive improvements and greater stress resilience.
timing: Best in the morning or before high-stress situations. Can be used 1–2× daily. Generally calming rather than stimulating — can be used later in the day if needed.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Anxiety / stress support: 0.25–0.5 mg (8–15 units) 1–2× daily. Mood balance / calm focus: 0.3–0.6 mg (10–20 units) morning or as needed. Conservative start: 0.17 mg (5 units) once daily for the first few days. At 3.33 mg/mL: 0.17 mg = 5 units, 0.5 mg = 15 units, 1 mg = 30 units.

name: Melanotan II
overview: Melanotan II is a synthetic analogue of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH) that activates melanocortin receptors to stimulate melanin production for enhanced tanning, while also producing libido-enhancing and mild appetite-suppressing effects through central melanocortin pathways.
mechanism: MT-2 binds to multiple melanocortin receptor subtypes: MC1R activation in skin cells drives melanin production for enhanced tanning with less UV exposure; MC4R activation in the hypothalamus produces the libido-enhancing and appetite-suppressing effects; MC5R contributes to sebaceous gland signaling. The tanning effect requires UV light exposure — MT-2 primes the skin to respond to UV more efficiently.
effects: Enhanced and faster tanning response with UV exposure, deeper and more even skin pigmentation, libido stimulation and enhanced arousal, mild appetite suppression in early use. Initial doses typically cause flushing and warmth — a normal response that diminishes with continued use.
results_timeline: First doses: warmth and flushing (normal). Days 3–5: skin begins darkening with UV exposure. Week 1–2: noticeable tan develops. Maintenance: color is sustained with reduced dosing frequency.
timing: Evening injection is preferred as the flushing response is better tolerated at rest. UV light exposure is required to activate tanning — plan sun or tanning sessions during the loading phase. Loading: daily for 7–14 days. Maintenance: 1–2× weekly.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Loading phase (build pigment): 0.25 mg (~8 units) daily, increasing to 0.5 mg (15 units) daily after tolerance is established. Run for 7–14 days while getting small UV exposure. Maintenance: 0.25–0.5 mg (8–15 units) 1–2× per week. At 3.33 mg/mL: 0.1 mg = 3 units, 0.25 mg = ~8 units, 0.5 mg = 15 units.

name: PT-141
overview: PT-141 (Bremelanotide) is a synthetic melanocortin agonist that acts centrally on brain pathways to enhance sexual arousal and libido in both men and women. Unlike PDE5 inhibitors, it works through the nervous system rather than vascular blood flow, making it effective through a fundamentally different mechanism.
mechanism: PT-141 activates melanocortin receptors MC3R and MC4R in the hypothalamus and limbic system, modulating dopaminergic and noradrenergic pathways involved in sexual arousal and motivation. This central nervous system mechanism is effective regardless of blood flow status, distinguishing it from vasodilatory approaches and making it useful in a broader range of research contexts.
effects: Enhanced sexual desire and arousal in men and women, improved libido and motivation, and potential mood elevation. Some users experience flushing and mild nausea at higher doses — manageable by dialing in the individual response dose. Effects vary significantly between individuals.
results_timeline: Onset within 30–60 minutes of injection. Peak effects at 1–2 hours post-injection. Effects can persist for several hours. Dial in the effective dose before the intended use window.
timing: Inject 30–60 minutes before desired effect. SubQ injection in abdomen or thigh. Use 1–2× per week maximum — not suitable for daily use. Start at the low end (0.25–0.5 mg) to assess individual response.

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Tolerance testing: 0.25–0.5 mg (5–10 units). Noticeable effect: 1 mg (20 units). Strong effect: 1.5–2 mg (30–40 units). Maximum range: 2.5 mg (50 units) — higher side effects possible. Use 1–2× per week only. Dial in your individual effective dose. At 5 mg/mL: 0.25 mg = 5 units, 1 mg = 20 units, 2 mg = 40 units.

name: 5-Amino-1MQ
overview: 5-Amino-1MQ (5-amino-1-methylquinolinium) is a small-molecule, selective inhibitor of Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT) studied for its potential to support fat metabolism, preserve lean muscle, and raise intracellular NAD+ levels.
mechanism: By inhibiting NNMT, an enzyme often overexpressed in adipose tissue during obesity, 5-Amino-1MQ spares nicotinamide for the NAD+ salvage pathway rather than letting it be methylated and cleared. The resulting rise in NAD+ activates SIRT1 pathways linked to mitochondrial biogenesis, fat oxidation, and metabolic flexibility.
effects: Preclinical models show reduced fat mass with preserved lean muscle, elevated NAD+ and SIRT1 activation, and improved grip strength in aged mice when paired with exercise. Generally well tolerated, with occasional mild headache, jitteriness, or injection-site reactions reported.
results_timeline: Not specified in source beyond general week-by-week dosing titration; long-term human outcome timelines are not established.
timing: Once or twice daily subcutaneous injection; twice-daily (BID) dosing may better sustain NNMT inhibition given a plasma half-life of roughly 3.8–6.9 hours. Morning administration is preferred, with BID protocols split AM/PM.
research_notes: Nature Medicine (2014) — Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase knockdown protects against diet-induced obesity, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24717514/ | PMC (2024) — Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase inhibition mitigates obesity-related metabolic dysfunctions, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11622326/ | Frontiers in Pharmacology (2024) — NNMT: a novel therapeutic target for metabolic syndrome, https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/pharmacology/articles/10.3389/fphar.2024.1410479/full | PubMed (2021) — LC-MS/MS assay for 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium: pharmacokinetic and oral bioavailability study, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34304009/

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Start at 2.5 mg (50 units) once daily for days 1–2 to assess tolerance, then move to the standard 5 mg (100 units) once daily, or split as 2.5 mg AM + 2.5 mg PM (50 units each). At 5 mg/mL: 1 unit = 0.05 mg. A single 10 mg vial lasts about 2–4 days at these doses.

name: Adamax
overview: Adamax is an adamantane-modified analog of Semax, a heptapeptide derivative of ACTH(4-10) studied for nootropic and neuroprotective properties. The adamantane modification is intended to improve blood-brain barrier penetration and stability compared to standard Semax.
mechanism: Structurally derived from Semax, Adamax is believed to increase brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) expression and enhance TrkB receptor sensitivity in the hippocampus, promoting neuroplasticity and neuronal survival. It also modulates dopamine and serotonin signaling and activates cAMP/CREB intracellular pathways linked to memory and stress resilience. The adamantane group is thought to add lipophilicity and enzymatic stability versus Semax, extending its effects and reducing dosing frequency needs.
effects: Reported benefits include improved learning and memory consolidation, mental clarity, neuroprotection under oxidative or ischemic stress, mood/stress resilience via dopamine-serotonin modulation, and increased neuroplasticity from elevated BDNF.
results_timeline: Weeks 1–2: adaptation period with subtle mood or clarity shifts. Weeks 3–6: more noticeable cognitive benefits (memory, focus). Weeks 6–12: sustained neuroplasticity effects with continued use.
timing: Once daily subcutaneous injection, preferably in the morning for consistency.
research_notes: PubMed — Semax: structure, mechanism, and therapeutic potential (ACTH analog overview), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11146367/ | Journal of Neurochemistry (2006) — Semax binds specific brain sites and increases BDNF protein in rat basal forebrain, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16635254/ | Medicinal Chemistry Letters (ACS) — Adamantane modifications enhance peptide stability and CNS penetration, https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsmedchemlett.8b00361 | PubMed — Clinical safety and tolerability of Semax in human studies (no major adverse effects), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15001158/

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Gradual titration: 300 mcg (9 units) daily weeks 1–2, 500 mcg (15 units) weeks 3–4, 750 mcg (23 units) weeks 5–6, up to 1000 mcg (30 units) weeks 7–8. Once daily, subcutaneous. Cycle 8–12 weeks (up to 16), followed by an equal off-cycle break. At 3.33 mg/mL, 1 unit is about 33.3 mcg — a 30- or 50-unit syringe helps precision at the lower starting doses.

name: Adipotide
overview: Adipotide is a synthetic peptidomimetic that targets prohibitin on blood vessels within white adipose tissue, delivering a pro-apoptotic sequence that destroys those vessels. Preclinical studies in obese rodents and primates showed substantial weight loss, but clinical development was discontinued after a Phase 1 human trial due to safety concerns.
mechanism: Adipotide binds prohibitin-1 on the vascular endothelium of white adipose tissue. Once bound, it delivers a pro-apoptotic peptide sequence that triggers destruction of those blood vessels, cutting off blood supply to nearby fat cells so they undergo apoptosis and are metabolized away.
effects: Preclinical studies reported roughly 30% body weight reduction in obese rodents and about 11% weight loss in obese primates after 4 weeks. The mechanism appears to selectively spare non-adipose tissue in animal models, but reversible kidney toxicity was observed at higher doses.
results_timeline: Weeks 1–2 correspond to the titration/tolerance-assessment phase. Primate studies observed roughly 11% weight loss by week 4. Rodent studies showed continued fat-loss trajectory through weeks 4–8.
timing: Once daily, any consistent time, with systematic rotation of injection sites.
research_notes: Science Translational Medicine (PubMed) — A peptidomimetic targeting white fat causes weight loss and improved insulin resistance in obese monkeys, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22072637/ | MD Anderson Cancer Center — Obese Monkeys Lose Weight on Drug that Attacks Blood Supply of Fat Cells, https://www.mdanderson.org/newsroom/obese-monkeys-lose-weight-on-drug-that-attacks-blood-supply-of-f.h00-158676579.html | Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals News — Dosing of First Patient with Anti-Obesity Treatment Adipotide in a Phase 1 Clinical Trial, https://arrowheadpharma.com/news-press/arrowhead-announces-dosing-of-first-patient-with-anti-obesity-treatment-adipotide-in-a-phase-1-clinical-trial/ | ScienceDaily — Obese monkeys lose weight on drug that attacks blood supply of fat cells, https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2011/11/111109143009.htm

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Cautious titration starting at 250 mcg (7.5 units) once daily, increasing by 250 mcg every 2 weeks as tolerated, up to 1000 mcg (30 units) daily by weeks 7–8. Keep cycles short (4–8 weeks) given the discontinued clinical development and preclinical kidney toxicity signal at higher doses. At 3.33 mg/mL, 1 unit is about 33.3 mcg.

name: AICAR
overview: AICAR (5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide ribonucleoside) is a research compound that activates AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK), earning it the nickname 'exercise pill' after studies showed sedentary animals given AICAR had dramatic endurance improvements. It is banned by WADA and not approved for human therapeutic use.
mechanism: AICAR is metabolized intracellularly to ZMP, an AMP analog that directly activates AMPK, the master regulator of cellular energy homeostasis. AMPK activation stimulates glucose uptake and fatty-acid oxidation in muscle and promotes mitochondrial biogenesis, mimicking the cellular effects of exercise or fasting.
effects: Sedentary mice treated with AICAR showed a 44% increase in endurance without training. Other observed effects in animal models include increased mitochondrial enzyme activity, improved insulin sensitivity, and potential protection against diabetic neuropathy. Generally well tolerated in studies with occasional mild injection-site reactions.
results_timeline: Weeks 1–2 are typically a titration/tolerance phase. Weeks 3–4 are when subjective energy or endurance shifts are commonly reported. Weeks 5–8 and beyond are where metabolic marker changes would be expected based on animal studies.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, at any consistent time; rotate injection sites daily.
research_notes: U.S. Anti-Doping Agency (USADA) — What Athletes Should Know About AICAR and Other Prohibited AMPK Activated Protein Kinase Activators, https://www.usada.org/spirit-of-sport/aicar-and-other-prohibited-amp-activated-protein-kinase-activators/ | PubMed (2017) — Doping control study of AICAR; endurance enhancement in sedentary animals (Drug Testing and Analysis), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28407446/ | PubMed (2013) — Acadesine (AICAR) Phase I/II study; maximum tolerated dose 210 mg/kg IV (Cancer Chemotherapy and Pharmacology), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23228986/ | MDPI (2024) — AICAR prevents and reverses diabetic polyneuropathy by regulating mitophagy, https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/26/1/80

doses.label: 50mg
doses.dosage_text: Conservative track: 1 mg (6 units) daily weeks 1–2, 2 mg (12 units) weeks 3–4, 3 mg (18 units) weeks 5–8. An advanced track exists in the literature going up to 5 mg (30 units) daily by weeks 7–12, but should only be used when explicitly supported by protocol design. At 16.7 mg/mL, 1 unit is about 167 mcg. Once daily subcutaneous.

name: AOD-9604
overview: AOD-9604 is a modified fragment of human growth hormone (Tyr-hGH 177–191) that retains GH's lipolytic (fat-reducing) properties without its growth-promoting effects. Clinical trials found it exhibits a placebo-like safety profile in obese adults.
mechanism: AOD-9604 binds adipose tissue and triggers breakdown of stored fat (lipolysis) while blocking fat cells from restocking (re-esterification). Chronic administration appears to upregulate beta-3 adrenergic receptors in fat tissue, reversing an obesity-associated suppression of these fat-burning receptors. Unlike full-length hGH, it does not meaningfully raise IGF-1 or worsen glucose tolerance.
effects: Clinical trials showed modest but statistically significant weight loss (about 2.6 kg vs 0.8 kg placebo over 12 weeks at 1 mg/day), with preferential loss of abdominal fat similar to low-dose hGH therapy. Placebo-like tolerability with no anti-AOD9604 antibodies detected. Preclinical work suggests possible cartilage repair and bone health benefits.
results_timeline: Weeks 1–4 are the titration period at 300 mcg. Weeks 5–8 establish the 500 mcg dose with fat-oxidation effects building. Weeks 8–12 is where clinically observed weight-loss differences became most apparent in trials.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous; morning administration on an empty stomach is common.
research_notes: Journal of Endocrinology and Metabolism (2014) — Safety and metabolism of AOD9604 as a nutraceutical ingredient, https://jofem.org/index.php/jofem/article/view/213/278 | Journal of Endocrinology and Metabolism (2013) — Safety and tolerability of AOD9604 in humans; no IGF-1 increase; placebo-like safety profile, https://www.jofem.org/index.php/jofem/article/view/157/194 | Gastroenterology — Drug treatment of the overweight patient; AOD-9604 RCT summary and clinical efficacy, https://www.gastrojournal.org/article/S0016-5085(07)00574-4/fulltext | Endocrinology (OUP) — Chronic AOD9604 treatment in obese and beta3-AR knockout mice; receptor upregulation, https://academic.oup.com/endo/article/142/12/5182/2988749

doses.label: 2mg
doses.dosage_text: 300 mcg (45 units) once daily for weeks 1–4, increasing to 500 mcg (75 units) once daily for weeks 5–12. At 0.667 mg/mL, 1 unit is about 6.67 mcg. Each 2 mg vial lasts roughly 4 days at 500 mcg/day, or 6–7 days at 300 mcg/day.

name: Ara-290
overview: Ara-290 (cibinetide) is an 11-amino-acid non-erythropoietic peptide derived from erythropoietin's helix-B domain. It selectively activates the innate repair receptor to promote anti-inflammatory and tissue-protective effects without stimulating red blood cell production.
mechanism: Ara-290 binds the innate repair receptor (IRR), a heterodimer of EPOR and CD131 (beta common subunit) distinct from the classical erythropoietin receptor. This triggers anti-apoptotic and anti-inflammatory signaling that protects tissue and supports repair. It has also been shown to inhibit TRPV1 ion channels, contributing to an analgesic effect independent of the IRR pathway.
effects: Clinical trials in diabetic neuropathy showed improvements in HbA1c, lipid profiles, and neuropathic pain scores at 4 mg/day. A sarcoidosis-associated small-fiber neuropathy trial found significant increases in corneal nerve fiber density (objective nerve regrowth) alongside symptom improvement versus placebo. Unlike full EPO, it does not stimulate erythropoiesis, avoiding related side effects. Well tolerated with occasional mild injection-site reactions.
results_timeline: Week 1 is a titration period at 2 mg to assess tolerance. Weeks 2 onward use the 4 mg/day maintenance dose that clinical trials (28-day protocols) were built around. Nerve fiber density changes in the sarcoidosis trial were measured over similar multi-week windows.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, at any consistent time; rotate injection sites.
research_notes: Molecular Medicine (2014) — Brines M. et al.: ARA 290 improves metabolic control and neuropathic symptoms in patients with type 2 diabetes, https://molmed.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.2119/molmed.2014.00215 | Molecular Medicine (2013) — Dahan A. et al.: ARA 290 improves symptoms in sarcoidosis-associated small nerve fiber loss and increases corneal nerve fiber density, https://molmed.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.2119/molmed.2013.00122 | ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT02039687) — Phase 2 study of ARA 290 on corneal nerve fiber density in sarcoidosis (1 mg vs 4 mg vs 8 mg daily SC for 28 days), https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT02039687 | Peptides Journal (2016) — Zhang W. et al.: ARA 290 relieves pathophysiological pain by targeting TRPV1 channel, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26774587/

doses.label: 16mg
doses.dosage_text: 2 mg (25 units) once daily during week 1 to assess tolerance, then 4 mg (50 units) once daily from week 2 onward — the dose used as the clinical target, since 8 mg showed no added benefit over 4 mg in trials. At 8 mg/mL, 1 unit is 80 mcg.

name: Cagrilintide
overview: Cagrilintide is a long-acting acylated analogue of the pancreatic hormone amylin, designed for once-weekly subcutaneous administration. It activates central amylin receptors to promote satiety, slow gastric emptying, and reduce food intake.
mechanism: Native amylin promotes satiation, slows gastric emptying, and inhibits postprandial glucagon secretion. Cagrilintide acts on central amylin receptors, particularly in the area postrema and hindbrain, to reduce appetite and energy intake. Lipid (fatty acid) modification of the molecule extends its half-life to roughly 160–195 hours, enabling once-weekly dosing.
effects: In a phase 2 trial, cagrilintide at 4.5 mg weekly produced approximately 10.8% body weight loss over 26 weeks versus 3.0% with placebo. Combined with semaglutide 2.4 mg (the CagriSema combination), phase 3 REDEFINE trials showed roughly 20% weight loss at 68 weeks, exceeding either agent alone. Side effects are primarily gastrointestinal — nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation — generally mild-to-moderate and transient, minimized by gradual titration.
results_timeline: Weeks 1–4 are titration, mostly managing GI side effects. Weeks 5–8 approach or reach the 4.5 mg maintenance dose. Weeks 8–26 is where the weight-loss curve builds, with ~10.8% total loss the 26-week phase 2 benchmark at full dose.
timing: Once weekly, subcutaneous, on a consistent day each week.
research_notes: The Lancet (2021) — Once-weekly cagrilintide for weight management: phase 2 dose-finding trial (Lau et al.), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34798060/ | N Engl J Med (2025) — REDEFINE 1: Coadministered cagrilintide and semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity, https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2502081 | N Engl J Med (2025) — REDEFINE 2: Cagrilintide-semaglutide in adults with overweight/obesity and type 2 diabetes, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40544432/ | J Med Chem (2021) — Development of cagrilintide: a long-acting amylin analogue, https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acs.jmedchem.1c00565

doses.label: 5mg
doses.dosage_text: Weekly titration: 0.6 mg (36 units) weeks 1–2, 1.2 mg (72 units) weeks 3–4, 2.4 mg (144 units, 1.44 mL) weeks 5–6, up to 4.5 mg (270 units, 2.70 mL) maintenance from week 7 on. At 1.67 mg/mL, 1 unit is about 16.7 mcg. Doses above 1 mL (100 units) require a 3 mL syringe with a subcutaneous needle rather than a standard U-100 insulin syringe.

name: Cartalax
overview: Cartalax is a synthetic tripeptide bioregulator (Ala-Glu-Asp, sequence 'AED') developed by Prof. Vladimir Khavinson at the St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology. It corresponds to a motif in type XI collagen and is studied in preclinical models for effects on fibroblast and chondrocyte function. Published human dosing data is limited; this profile draws on preclinical and observational sources.
mechanism: As one of the Khavinson bioregulator peptides, Cartalax is proposed to interact with DNA and modulate gene expression at nanomolar concentrations. In fibroblast and chondrocyte culture studies it has been reported to upregulate Ki-67 (a proliferation marker), increase SIRT-1/SIRT-6 expression, reduce p53 and caspase-3 activity (pro-apoptotic signals), and inhibit MMP-9 synthesis, an enzyme linked to extracellular matrix breakdown.
effects: Preclinical data suggest support for fibroblast proliferation, reduced markers of cellular senescence in aged cell cultures, and modulation of extracellular matrix homeostasis via MMP-9 inhibition and collagen-related gene expression. Khavinson bioregulator peptides are generally described as well tolerated in observational use, with occasional mild injection-site reactions possible.
results_timeline: Not specified in source with clinical confidence — the site notes evidence is limited to in vitro/rodent studies rather than human trial timelines.
timing: Once daily, any consistent time, with rotation of injection sites.
research_notes: Neuroendocrinology Letters (2002) — Khavinson VK. Peptides and Ageing. Overview of bioregulatory peptide development and geroprotective mechanisms, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12374906/ | Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine (2016) — Lin'kova NS et al. Peptide Regulation of Skin Fibroblast Functions during Their Aging In Vitro, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27259496/ | International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2023) — Linkova N, Khavinson V et al. Peptide Regulation of Chondrogenic Stem Cell Differentiation, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37175656/ | Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine (2014) — Khavinson VK et al. Peptides regulate the expression of signaling molecules in kidney cell cultures during in vitro aging, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24958378/

doses.label: 20mg
doses.dosage_text: Gradual titration: 2 mg (30 units) daily weeks 1–2, 3 mg (45 units) weeks 3–4, 4 mg (60 units) weeks 5–8, 5 mg (75 units) weeks 9–12. At 6.67 mg/mL, 1 unit is about 66.7 mcg. Note this schedule is extrapolated from preclinical fibroblast/chondrocyte research, not established human dosing data.

name: Cerebrolysin
overview: Cerebrolysin is a porcine brain-derived peptide preparation containing low-molecular-weight neuropeptides and free amino acids that crosses the blood-brain barrier to support neuronal survival. It mimics endogenous neurotrophic factors such as NGF and BDNF and modulates neuroinflammatory mediators.
mechanism: Composed of biologically active neuropeptides and amino acids under 10 kDa in molecular weight, Cerebrolysin crosses the blood-brain barrier and exerts neurotrophic effects similar to nerve growth factor (NGF) and brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF). Preclinical studies show neuroprotective effects including reduced infarct size and improved recovery in ischemia models.
effects: Meta-analyses of human dementia trials suggest modest cognitive benefits with daily dosing; stroke trial results have been more variable. Generally well tolerated short-term, with occasional mild injection-site reactions, dizziness, headache, or gastrointestinal upset reported.
results_timeline: Week 1 begins at the 20 mg starting dose as a single daily injection. Weeks 2–4 involve titrating up to the 32 mg/day maintenance dose split AM/PM. From week 4 onward, at the maintenance dose, cognitive or neuroprotective effects described in trial literature would be expected to build.
timing: Once or twice daily; doses above 20 mg are split into AM and PM injections at different sites.
research_notes: International Peptide Society — Cerebrolysin Monograph: mechanism, pharmacology, and clinical applications (2018), https://peptidesociety.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/08/Cerebrolysin-Monograph-Complete-1.pdf | Alzheimer's Drug Discovery Foundation — Cerebrolysin cognitive vitality report: evidence synthesis and safety overview (2016), https://www.alzdiscovery.org/uploads/cognitive_vitality_media/addf-cerebrolysin-full-report.pdf | Zhang et al., Med Sci Monit (PMC) — Meta-analysis of Cerebrolysin efficacy and safety in acute ischemic stroke (2017), https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5474547/ | Kurkin et al., PLOS One — Neuroprotective action of Cerebrolysin in acute/chronic brain ischemia in rats (2021), https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0254493

doses.label: 60mg
doses.dosage_text: Week 1: 20 mg (100 units) once daily as a single injection. Week 2: 24 mg/day split as 60 units AM + 60 units PM. Week 3: 28 mg/day split as 70 units AM + 70 units PM. Week 4+: 32 mg/day split as 80 units AM + 80 units PM. At 20 mg/mL, 1 unit = 0.2 mg. Doses exceeding 100 units (1 mL) must be split into two separate injections at different sites.

name: Chonluten
overview: Chonluten is a short bioregulatory tripeptide (Glu-Asp-Gly) from the Khavinson peptide bioregulator family, studied specifically for its effects on bronchopulmonary tissue and inflammatory signaling in monocyte/macrophage cell models.
mechanism: Research in THP-1 monocyte/macrophage cell models suggests Chonluten modulates proliferative activity and inflammatory pathways at nanomolar concentrations, consistent with its classification as a tissue-specific bioregulator peptide targeting lung and bronchial tissue.
effects: May support bronchopulmonary tissue function and help regulate inflammatory and proliferative pathways in respiratory-relevant cell models. Short peptides in this bioregulator class are generally well tolerated, with occasional mild injection-site irritation.
results_timeline: As a slow-titration protocol run over 8-16 weeks, most users don't expect noticeable effects until well into the schedule, once doses reach the 2-4 mg/day range around weeks 9-16.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, any consistent time of day with sites rotated between abdomen, thighs, and upper arms.
research_notes: Peptides Regulating Proliferative Activity and Inflammatory Pathways in the Monocyte/Macrophage THP-1 Cell Line, Int J Mol Sci (2022), https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms23073607 | Overcoming Oral Cavity Barriers for Peptide Delivery Using Advanced Pharmaceutical Techniques and Nano-Formulation Platforms, Biomedicines (2025), https://doi.org/10.3390/biomedicines13112735 | Therapeutic peptides: current applications and future directions, Signal Transduction and Targeted Therapy (2022), https://www.nature.com/articles/s41392-022-00904-4

doses.label: 20mg
doses.dosage_text: Once-daily subcutaneous injection, titrated gradually from 250 mcg (3.75 units) up to 4 mg (60 units) over 8-16 weeks, increasing roughly every 1-2 weeks. At 6.67 mg/mL: 250 mcg = 3.75 units, 1 mg = 15 units, 4 mg = 60 units. Use a 30- or 50-unit syringe for the smallest early doses.

name: CJC-1295 DAC
overview: CJC-1295 DAC is a long-acting growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analog modified with a Drug Affinity Complex (DAC) that binds to serum albumin, extending its half-life to roughly 6-8 days. This allows once-weekly dosing rather than the daily injections needed for standard GHRH analogs.
mechanism: The DAC modification causes the peptide to bind covalently to albumin in the bloodstream after injection, dramatically slowing its clearance. It then acts on GHRH receptors on pituitary somatotrope cells to amplify natural, pulsatile GH synthesis and release, while preserving the normal frequency and amplitude of GH pulses rather than flooding the body with GH the way exogenous HGH does.
effects: Sustained elevation of GH (2-10 fold) and IGF-1 (1.5-3 fold) supporting muscle growth, recovery, and lean body composition; improved fat metabolism with potential reduction in visceral fat; improved slow-wave sleep quality; preserved natural GH pulsatility. Mild flushing, injection-site reactions, water retention, or headache can occur but serious effects are uncommon at standard doses.
results_timeline: Sleep quality changes can appear within 1-2 weeks. Recovery and fat metabolism effects tend to show up around weeks 3-6, with more visible body composition changes by weeks 6-12. IGF-1 elevation can persist up to 28 days after repeated dosing.
timing: Once weekly, subcutaneous, ideally in the evening before bed on an empty stomach (2+ hours after eating) to align with natural nocturnal GH release and avoid blood-sugar-related blunting.
research_notes: Teichman et al., Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone and insulin-like growth factor I secretion by CJC-1295, a long-acting growth hormone-releasing hormone analog, Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, https://academic.oup.com/jcem/article/91/3/799/2843268 | DAC technology: albumin-binding for extended peptide half-life, Clinical Pharmacology & Therapeutics, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16988206/ | Activation of the GH/IGF-1 axis by CJC-1295 and serum protein profile changes, Growth Hormone & IGF Research, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1096637409000409 | GHRH analogs and preservation of pulsatile GH secretion patterns, Endocrine Reviews, https://academic.oup.com/edrv/article/19/6/717/2530852

doses.label: 2mg
doses.dosage_text: 2 mg (100 units, the full vial) injected once weekly, ideally in the evening. A more conservative 1 mg (50 units) weekly option exists for those wanting a lower starting point. Typical cycles run 8-12 weeks with an optional 4-week break between cycles. At 2 mg/mL: 1 mg = 50 units, 2 mg = 100 units.

name: CJC-1295 NO DAC
overview: CJC-1295 NO DAC, also known as Modified GRF 1-29 or Mod GRF, is a short-acting synthetic 29-amino-acid GHRH analog. Unlike the DAC version, it lacks the albumin-binding tail, giving it a short half-life of roughly 30 minutes and producing a sharper, more physiologic GH pulse.
mechanism: Four amino acid substitutions (D-Ala2, Gln8, Ala15, Leu27) protect the peptide from enzymatic degradation. It binds to GHRH receptors on pituitary somatotrophs to stimulate synthesis and pulsatile release of endogenous growth hormone, preserving the body's physiologic feedback loop rather than producing supraphysiologic GH levels the way exogenous GH would.
effects: Stimulates pulsatile GH release and raises IGF-1 with chronic use; may support improved body composition (more lean mass, less fat mass), better recovery, sleep quality, and energy levels. Generally well tolerated, with occasional injection-site reactions, transient flushing, or headache.
results_timeline: Early weeks (1-2) are mostly adjustment with possible early sleep changes. Recovery and energy improvements tend to show around weeks 3-6, with body composition and IGF-1-related changes becoming more noticeable from week 5 through week 12.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, typically at bedtime and in a fasted state (2+ hours since last meal) to align with and not blunt the natural nocturnal GH pulse.
research_notes: Modified GRF (1-29): structure, amino acid substitutions, and pharmacokinetics, Wikipedia, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CJC-1295 | Teichman et al., Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone and IGF-I by CJC-1295 in healthy adults, Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism (2006), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16352683/ | Alba et al., Once-daily CJC-1295 normalizes growth in GHRH knockout mice, American Journal of Physiology (2006), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16822960/ | Veldhuis et al., Pulsatile GH secretion persists during continuous CJC-1295 stimulation, Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism (2006), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17018654/

doses.label: 5mg
doses.dosage_text: Once-daily subcutaneous injection at bedtime, titrated from 100 mcg (6 units) up to 250-300 mcg (15-18 units) over 8-12 weeks, increasing roughly every 1-2 weeks. At 1.67 mg/mL: 100 mcg = 6 units, 300 mcg = 18 units. Best taken fasted to maximize GH response.

name: Cortagen
overview: Cortagen (Ala-Glu-Asp-Pro) is a synthetic tetrapeptide derived from amino acid analysis of the brain cortex preparation Cortexin, part of the Khavinson bioregulator peptide class. It is studied for neuroprotective and neuroregenerative properties, particularly peripheral nerve regeneration and cognitive function.
mechanism: Cortagen acts as a gene expression modulator and epigenetic regulator, influencing synthesis of proteins involved in neuronal growth, differentiation, and stress resistance. Preclinical studies show it can upregulate neurotrophic factors, reduce oxidative stress markers, and accelerate peripheral nerve regeneration.
effects: Supports peripheral nerve regeneration with improved nerve fiber growth rate and conduction velocity (27-40% improvement in injury models); neuroprotective in cerebral ischemia models by reducing lipid peroxidation and preserving antioxidant enzyme activity; may enhance cognitive function through synaptic plasticity and neurite outgrowth. Generally well tolerated with no significant adverse effects reported in animal studies at therapeutic doses.
results_timeline: Weeks 1-2 are a titration phase at the lower 1 mg dose with limited expected effect. Once the full 2 mg dose is reached in weeks 3-4, this is where most of the reported research effects would be expected to apply, continuing through an optional 8-week extension.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, morning administration preferred over evening.
research_notes: Effect of tetrapeptide cortagen on regeneration of sciatic nerve, PubMed, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11276314/ | Cortexin and cortagen as correcting agents in functional and metabolic disorders in the brain in chronic ischemia, PubMed, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21476278/ | Elucidation of the effect of brain cortex tetrapeptide Cortagen on gene expression in mouse heart by microarray, PubMed (Neuroendocrinology Letters), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15159690/ | Peptide bioregulation of aging: results and prospects, PubMed (Biogerontology), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19830585/

doses.label: 20mg
doses.dosage_text: Once-daily morning subcutaneous injection: 1 mg (15 units) for weeks 1-2, stepping up to 2 mg (30 units) for weeks 3-4. Standard cycle is 4 weeks, extendable to 8 weeks. At 6.67 mg/mL: 1 mg = 15 units, 2 mg = 30 units.

name: DSIP
overview: DSIP (Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide) is a naturally occurring nonapeptide first isolated from rabbit brain tissue, studied for its effects on slow-wave sleep architecture and stress modulation.
mechanism: DSIP is thought to influence sleep by modulating GABAergic transmission and interacting with opioid receptor systems, increasing delta-wave (slow-wave) sleep duration without significantly altering REM architecture. It has also shown stress-protective and adaptogenic properties in various models, potentially through modulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis.
effects: May promote deeper, more restorative slow-wave sleep and improve subjective sleep quality; potential stress-protective and anxiolytic-like effects; some studies indicate it does not induce tolerance with continued use. Generally well tolerated, though long-term safety data is limited.
results_timeline: Week 1 is mostly adjustment to injections with possibly subtle early changes. Sleep depth and quality shifts tend to appear around weeks 2-3, with the most consistent sleep and stress-resilience benefits reported from week 4 through week 8.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, typically in the evening before bedtime.
research_notes: Schoenenberger GA, Monnier M, Characterization of a delta-electroencephalogram (-sleep)-inducing peptide, Proc Natl Acad Sci USA (1977), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16592440/ | Schneider-Helmert D, Schoenenberger GA, Effects of DSIP in man: multifunctional psychophysiological properties, Neuropsychobiology (1983), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/6633115/ | Sudakov KV et al., Delta-sleep-inducing peptide sequelae: stress protective effect, Ann N Y Acad Sci (2004), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15677401/ | Graf MV, Kastin AJ, Delta-sleep-inducing peptide (DSIP): a review, Neurosci Biobehav Rev (1984), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/6392951/

doses.label: 5mg
doses.dosage_text: Once-daily subcutaneous injection before bed, titrated from 100 mcg (6 units) in week 1 up to 250-300 mcg (15-18 units) by weeks 4-8. An anecdotal extended range up to 400-500 mcg (24-30 units) exists but is not well studied — formal research has mostly used doses up to 300 mcg/day. At 1.67 mg/mL: 100 mcg = 6 units, 300 mcg = 18 units.

name: Epitalon
overview: Epitalon (also called Epithalon) is a synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) developed from pineal gland research, studied for its geroprotective potential — particularly telomerase activation, melatonin regulation, and longevity outcomes in both animal and human studies.
mechanism: Epitalon's primary mechanism is telomerase activation, which promotes telomere elongation in cells, allowing continued division beyond the normal Hayflick limit in vitro. It also stimulates nocturnal melatonin release from the pineal gland, normalizing circadian hormone rhythms, along with reported antioxidant and immune-modulating effects.
effects: Supports telomere maintenance and cellular longevity through telomerase activation; restores healthy melatonin production and circadian rhythm regulation; extended lifespan and reduced tumor incidence in animal models; a 12-year human follow-up study showed improved cardiovascular outcomes and reduced mortality. Generally well tolerated with no serious adverse events reported in long-term studies.
results_timeline: Sleep and circadian rhythm shifts may begin within the first week as melatonin regulation kicks in. The core 20-day dosing window is what most research protocols are built around. Telomere and longevity-related effects are long-horizon outcomes not felt day to day.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, preferably in the evening or at bedtime to align with natural melatonin rhythms, for 20 consecutive days followed by a 4-6 month break.
research_notes: Overview of Epitalon: Highly Bioactive Pineal Tetrapeptide with Promising Properties, Int J Mol Sci (MDPI) (2025), https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/26/6/2691 | Epitalon increases telomere length in human cell lines through telomerase upregulation, PMC/Biogerontology, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12411320/ | Synthetic tetrapeptide Epitalon restores neuroendocrine regulation in senescent monkeys, Neuroendocrinology Letters (PubMed), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11524632/ | Geroprotective effect of epithalamine in elderly with accelerated aging (12-year RCT), Bull Exp Biol Med (PubMed), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17426848/

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: 5 mg (100 units, full vial) injected once daily for 20 consecutive days, then a 4-6 month break before repeating (roughly 2 cycles per year). An alternative schedule uses 10 mg/day for 10 days for the same total cycle dose of 100 mg. At 5 mg/mL: 1 mg = 20 units, 5 mg = 100 units.

name: FOXO4-DRI
overview: FOXO4-DRI (also known as Proxofim) is a synthetic D-retro-inverso peptide designed to selectively induce apoptosis in senescent cells by disrupting the FOXO4-p53 protein interaction. It is one of the most-discussed senolytic peptides in longevity research, though it currently has no human clinical data behind it.
mechanism: In normal cells, FOXO4 binds p53 and sequesters it in the nucleus. FOXO4-DRI disrupts this interaction specifically in senescent cells, causing p53 to translocate to the mitochondria and trigger apoptotic cell death in aged, dysfunctional cells while sparing healthy ones. Its D-retro-inverso structure enhances resistance to enzymatic breakdown.
effects: Animal studies show selective clearance of senescent cells, improved tissue regeneration, reduced inflammation, and enhanced organ function. In aged mice, treatment improved testosterone levels and cellular health markers. No human clinical safety or efficacy data currently exist, so tolerability in humans is uncharacterized.
results_timeline: Not specified in source for humans — all reported tissue-level improvements come from mouse studies measured over weeks to months of dosing, not days, and there is no established human timeline.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous, any consistent time, with doses gradually titrated upward over 8-16 weeks.
research_notes: FOXO4-DRI induces keloid senescent fibroblast apoptosis by promoting nuclear exclusion of upregulated p53-serine 15 phosphorylation, Nature Communications Biology, https://www.nature.com/articles/s42003-025-07738-0 | FOXO4-DRI alleviates age-related testosterone secretion insufficiency by targeting senescent Leydig cells in aged mice, Aging (Albany NY), https://www.aging-us.com/article/102682/text | FOXO4 peptide targets myofibroblast, ameliorates bleomycin-induced pulmonary fibrosis in mice, Journal of Cellular and Molecular Medicine, https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jcmm.17333 | FOXO4-DRI safety note: no human clinical dosing data exist yet, Aging (Albany NY) — Methods & Protocols, https://www.aging-us.com/article/102682/text#materialsandmethods

doses.label: 10mg
doses.dosage_text: Once-daily subcutaneous injection, titrated from 250 mcg (7.5 units) for weeks 1-4, up to 375 mcg (11 units) for weeks 5-8, then 500 mcg (15 units) for weeks 9-16. All doses stay under 0.2 mL at this dilution. At 3.33 mg/mL: 250 mcg = 7.5 units, 500 mcg = 15 units.

name: GHRP-2
overview: GHRP-2 (Growth Hormone-Releasing Peptide-2) is a synthetic hexapeptide that stimulates potent, dose-dependent growth hormone release by activating ghrelin receptors — a distinct mechanism from GHRH analogs like CJC-1295.
mechanism: GHRP-2 binds to and activates ghrelin (GH secretagogue) receptors, triggering pulsatile GH release from the pituitary in a dose-dependent manner. Clinical studies in GH-deficient children demonstrated meaningful GH increases even at low doses over months of treatment.
effects: Stimulates robust pulsatile GH release that may support favorable body composition and metabolic changes. In combination with GHRH analogs, can meaningfully increase IGF-1 with strict compliance. Generally well tolerated with a near placebo-like safety profile; transient effects may include mild hunger or flushing.
results_timeline: Weeks 1-2 at the starting dose are mostly about gauging tolerance. Weeks 3-4 involve stepping the dose up, where appetite and energy changes may appear. From week 5 through week 16 at the full dose, body composition changes are most likely to become noticeable with consistent use.
timing: Once daily, subcutaneous; evening administration may align with nocturnal GH rhythms. Frequent dosing (e.g., every 2 hours) causes rapid receptor desensitization, so once-daily is preferred over multiple daily injections.
research_notes: Effects of GHRP-2 on GH release and growth performance in swine, Domest Anim Endocrinol, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10793268/ | Effects of eight months treatment with graded doses of GHRP-2 in GH-deficient children, J Clin Endocrinol Metab, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9661608/ | Five-day treatment with daily subcutaneous GHRP-2: response attenuation and IGF-I effects in healthy young men, Eur J Endocrinol, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9820615/ | GH/IGF-1 response to acute and chronic GHRP-2, GHRH 1-44, and their combination in older adults, Endocrine, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11322505/

doses.label: 5mg
doses.dosage_text: Once-daily subcutaneous injection, titrated from 100 mcg (6 units) for weeks 1-2, to 150 mcg (9 units) for weeks 3-4, up to 200 mcg (12 units) for weeks 5-16 as the practical ceiling for most use. Advanced users occasionally push to 250-300 mcg (15-18 units), though this offers limited added benefit. At 1.67 mg/mL: 100 mcg = 6 units, 200 mcg = 12 units.

name: GHRP-6
overview: GHRP-6 (Growth Hormone-Releasing Peptide 6) is a synthetic hexapeptide and potent growth hormone secretagogue that binds the ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a). Its short half-life means it's typically dosed multiple times per day rather than once, and it also carries a separate tissue-protective signaling pathway distinct from its GH effects.
mechanism: GHRP-6 mimics ghrelin by binding GHS-R1a in the pituitary and hypothalamus, triggering pulsatile GH release while reducing somatostatin's inhibitory brake — though the body's natural feedback loop still keeps GH within physiologic ranges. Separately, it interacts with CD36 receptors on immune and muscle cells, activating PI3K/Akt cell-survival signaling that underlies reported cardioprotective, neuroprotective, and anti-fibrotic effects in preclinical models.
effects: Elevated GH and IGF-1 supporting lean mass and fat loss over time; improved sleep architecture (more slow-wave sleep) and recovery; appetite stimulation from ghrelin-receptor activation; preclinical tissue-protective effects including reduced scar formation, cardioprotection, and neuroprotection. Side effects include transient mild cortisol/ACTH increases, appetite stimulation, water retention, and occasional injection-site irritation or brief flushing.
results_timeline: Weeks 1-2 involve adjusting to the three-times-daily schedule, with appetite increase most noticeable here. Recovery and sleep architecture improvements tend to show around weeks 3-6, with muscle/fat composition changes and accumulated tissue-protective effects appearing from week 6 through week 12 of consistent dosing.
timing: Three times daily, subcutaneous, spaced at least 4 hours apart, on an empty stomach (2-3 hours after eating, waiting 30 minutes before the next meal) to maximize GH response.
research_notes: Growth hormone-releasing peptides: comprehensive review of GHRP mechanisms and effects, European Journal of Endocrinology (1997), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9186261/ | Synthetic GHRPs: historical appraisal of cytoprotective effects and mechanisms, Clinical Medicine Insights: Cardiology (2017), https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5392015/ | Pharmacokinetic study of GHRP-6 in healthy male volunteers, European Journal of Pharmaceutical Sciences (2013), https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23099431/ | GHRP-6 prevents cutaneous hypertrophic scarring: proteome study, International Wound Journal (2018), https://doi.org/10.1111/iwj.12895

doses.label: 2mg
doses.dosage_text: Three-times-daily subcutaneous injections, spaced 4+ hours apart, titrated from 100 mcg (15 units) per shot in weeks 1-2 (300 mcg/day total), to 200 mcg (30 units) per shot in weeks 3-4 (600 mcg/day total), up to 300 mcg (45 units) per shot in weeks 5-12 (900 mcg/day total). At 0.67 mg/mL: 100 mcg = 15 units, 300 mcg = 45 units.

name: Gonadorelin
overview: Gonadorelin is a synthetic version of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH), the decapeptide the hypothalamus naturally secretes in pulses. It's used in research protocols to reactivate the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis by prompting the pituitary to release LH and FSH.
mechanism: Gonadorelin binds GnRH receptors on pituitary gonadotrophs, triggering pulsatile release of LH and FSH. LH drives Leydig cell testosterone production in men and ovulation in women, while FSH supports spermatogenesis and follicle development. Because it mimics the body's own short, pulsatile GnRH signal (rather than a continuous agonist), brief periodic dosing preserves normal signaling instead of desensitizing receptors.
effects: Supports endogenous testosterone production via LH stimulation, may help maintain testicular size/function during exogenous testosterone therapy, and can promote spermatogenesis and fertility in hypogonadotropic hypogonadism when dosed in a pulsatile pattern. Generally well tolerated; occasional mild injection-site irritation, transient headache, or flushing have been reported.
results_timeline: Not specified in source in week-by-week detail; effects on LH/FSH stimulation are described as occurring with each pulsatile dose, with functional outcomes (testosterone, spermatogenesis) building over the 4-8 week protocol.
timing: Subcutaneous injections 2-3 times weekly on non-consecutive days (e.g., Mon/Wed/Fri) to mimic pulsatile GnRH release; morning injections preferred. An advanced/post-cycle approach uses daily dosing but strictly limited to 14 consecutive days to avoid receptor desensitization.
research_notes: GnRH agonists: gonadorelin, leuprolide, and nafarelin - overview of pharmacology and clinical applications, PubMed, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/1835275/ | Gonadorelin injection routes and indications for LH/FSH stimulation testing, Mayo Clinic, https://www.mayoclinic.org/drugs-supplements/gonadorelin-intravenous-route-injection-route/description/drg-20067426 | Gonadotropin-releasing hormone analogs: pulsatile vs. continuous effects on FSH/LH, PMC, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4229791/ | Pulsatile gonadorelin pump induces earlier spermatogenesis in congenital HH men, PMC, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6775549/

doses.label: 2mg
doses.dosage_text: 50-150 mcg (5-15 units) per injection, 2-3 times weekly on non-consecutive days, for a 4-8 week cycle. At 1 mg/mL, each unit equals 10 mcg. An advanced post-cycle approach uses 200 mcg (20 units) daily for a week, tapering to 100 mcg (10 units), but daily dosing should not exceed 14 consecutive days.

name: HCG
overview: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin (HCG) is a glycoprotein hormone that mimics luteinizing hormone (LH), used in research and clinical protocols to maintain testicular function and fertility, especially alongside testosterone replacement therapy or after suppression from anabolic steroid use.
